note: Checkpoint written mid-analysis due to context budget. Two gray areas resolved interactively before /clear. Remaining areas need discussion in next session. Do NOT re-ask the two completed areas.
areas_remaining: Exit Code Semantics for Partial Batch

Failure Classification Policy (revises D.question: Stop-on-first-failure (D-10 from Phase 2) or skip-and-continue across the batch?
Failure Classification Policy (revises D.answer: Differentiated policy by failure class. Skip-and-continue for pre-flight / item-specific failures (gas estimation exhausted retries, simulation revert on that item). Stop-and-exit-non-zero for post-submission / systemic failures (null or timed-out receipt, RPC connection loss, balance drops below threshold mid-batch). Stop-and-exit-zero on SIGTERM/SIGINT (graceful shutdown). Revises Phase 2 D-10 blanket stop-on-first-failure.
Failure Classification Policy (revises D.rationale: D-10's stop-on-first-failure creates a DoS risk in the stateless architecture — a single poison item (e.g., already-revoked index, unexpected contract state) would block every downstream item on every subsequent run. Differentiating by failure class preserves D-10's safety for systemic failures (where continuing burns gas on doomed txs) while unblocking the batch when only one item is problematic. Matches the intent of success criteria: SC-1 (gas est → retries then skip) is item-specific; SC-2 (receipt timeout → exit non-zero) is post-submission; SC-3 (empty wallet → exit) is systemic; SC-4 (SIGTERM → clean exit) is graceful.
options_presented: Switch to skip-and-continue for all failures

Signal Handling Scope.question: Handle only SIGTERM (per TXSAFE-04 / SC-4), or SIGTERM + SIGINT?
Signal Handling Scope.answer: Both SIGTERM and SIGINT wired to the same graceful shutdown handler. On signal: set shuttingDown flag, let any in-flight waitForTransactionReceipt complete, skip remaining actions, emit RunSummary, exit 0.
Signal Handling Scope.rationale: Ctrl+C (SIGINT) is the common developer interrupt during local runs; ignoring it would leave dev interrupts killing the process mid-tx uncleanly. Zero extra cost to register both on the same handler. REQUIREMENTS.md TXSAFE-04 already explicitly says 'SIGTERM/SIGINT'.
